title: Dean Jinzler
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Jinzler}} {{Expand|[[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']], ''[[Survivor's Quest]]'' }} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image= |name=Dean Jinzler |homeworld=[[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]][[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass=Fifteen kilos more than [[Bearsh]]''[[Survivor's Quest]]'' |hair=[[Color/Legends|Dark]], later silver |feathers= |eyes=Gray |skin= |cyber= |families=[[Jinzler family]] |parents=*[[Dean and Lorana Jinzler's father|Dean and Lorana Jinzler's father]] *[[Dean and Lorana Jinzler's mother]] |partners= |siblings=*[[Lorana Jinzler]] *2 other siblings |children= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] **[[Senate Support Services]] *[[Jinzler family]] *[[Car'das/Legends|Car'das]] *[[Empire of the Hand]] **[[Outbound Flight Colony]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I'm not an ambassador, Guardian. I'm an electronics technician. Like my father before me.|Dean Jinzler to Jorad Pressor|}} '''Dean Jinzler''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Technician/Legends|technician]], who was the youngest of the four [[Jinzler family|Jinzler children]]. ==Biography== {{Quote|Just tell him that his sister was thinking about him, hoping that someday he'll be able to let go of his anger.|Lorana Jinzler|}} Dean's [[Parent/Legends|parents]] were both employees at the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. After his older [[Gender/Legends|sister]] [[Lorana Jinzler|Lorana]] was born and taken in by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], his parents were dismissed in accordance with the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code]] regarding emotional attachments and relationships. However, his parents kept track of Lorana's Jedi training via a friend who still worked at the Temple. Dean was the youngest of the four [[Jinzler family|Jinzler children]] and grew up loathing his elder sister for being a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. Dean's parents were truly proud of Lorana, which caused Dean to feel jealous and unloved. In actuality, his parents offered him the same love and encouragement as they gave Lorana, but Dean blocked this out and created a false belief that grew with him over the years. Dean eventually became a skilled [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Technician/Legends|technician]] and was part of the ground crew who worked on the construction of ''[[Outbound Flight]]''.{{CSWECite|II|165|Jinzler, Dean}} Lorana and Dean met for the first and only time on [[Yaga Minor/Legends|Yaga Minor]], just before Lorana embarked upon the ill-fated [[Outbound Flight Project|Outbound Flight]] mission, which led to her [[Death/Legends|death]]. During their heated conversation in ''[[Outbound Flight]]''{{'s}} mess hall, Dean inspected Lorana's green-bladed [[Lorana Jinzler's lightsaber|lightsaber]] and revealed to her that was a gift from their parents. Their parents gave the [[Amethyst/Legends|Amethyst]] to a group of [[Coruscanti/Legends|citizens]] from the mid-levels of the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] who were once rescued by Lorana and her [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Jorus C'baoth]]. In turn the citizens gave her the amethyst for her lightsaber on behalf of her and Dean's [[Dean and Lorana Jinzler's mother|mother]] and [[Dean and Lorana Jinzler's father|father]]. Dean's jealousy convinced Lorana to travel on ''Outbound Flight''. Later, after ''Outbound FLight'' was severely damaged during the [[Battle for Outbound Flight]], Lorana instructed [[Jorj Car'das]] to find Dean and tell him to let go of his hatred towards her and their parents. Like his father, Dean trained as an electronics technician, achieving certificates in comm tech, [[Droid/Legends|droid]] maintenance and [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] tech and working in the [[Senate Support Services]]. Dean traveled [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] frequently during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], spending a brief time in the [[Hadar sector/Legends|Hadar sector]] following the Wars, during which he learned an ingenious trick for exterminating infestations of [[Conduit worm/Legends|conduit worms]]. Two years after the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] signed their historic peace treaty, Dean joined [[Talon Karrde]]'s operation. Following advice from Car'das, Dean transferred to the [[Comra/Legends|Comra]] listening station, where he intercepted a transmission coming from [[Nirauan/Legends|Nirauan]], for the attention of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke]] and [[Mara Jade Skywalker]]. The [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] had located the wreckage of Outbound Flight, and Dean, masquerading as a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] Ambassador, [[Expedition to Outbound Flight|joined the expedition]]. During the mission Luke and Mara recovered Lorana's damaged [[Lorana Jinzler's lightsaber]] from the remains of [[Dreadnaught-One]], but later used it to destroy a [[droideka]]. At first, Dean thought he needed Lorana's lightsaber, as his last remaining material connection to his sister. However, the journey to the place where Lorana sacrificed her life helped Dean come to terms with the bitter feelings he once had for his sister. As a result, Dean was able to accept the loss and felt it was fitting that the weapon was sacrificed to protect the survivors of Outbound Flight, just as Lorana had given her own life. He also came to terms with the lie he'd been living over the past sixty years, finally accepting himself for who he was. After the mission, Dean retired to the [[Empire of the Hand]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|I hear you, human. ''[…]'' You are as big a fool as they are. Stay back, or die now in agony instead of later in cold and darkness.''"
"''You're the one who's going to die.|Bearsh and Jinzler|}} Dean Jinzler was a [[Human/Legends|human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], with short-cropped [[Color/Legends|dark]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], which later turned silver, and gray [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. Lorana referred to Dean as being medium in height and build. He was taller than [[Bearsh]], and weighed at least fifteen kilos more than the [[Vagaari/Legends|Vagaari]]. Dean was jealous of his older sister Lorana for being a Jedi and felt unloved by his parents. After seeing Outbound Flight's wreckage in person, he realized he was holding a grudge against Lorana, and came to terms with his past, letting go of his anger. ==Behind the scenes== Dean Jinzler first appeared in the [[2004]] novel ''[[Survivor's Quest]]'' written by [[Timothy Zahn]]. ==Appearances== *[[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] {{Ab|Outbound Flight (unabridged audiobook)}} *[[Outbound Flight (abridged audiobook)|''Outbound Flight'' abridged audiobook]] *''[[Survivor's Quest]]'' {{Ab|Survivor's Quest (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book and [[Survivor's Quest (abridged audiobook)|audiobook]]}} *[[Survivor's Quest (abridged audiobook)|''Survivor's Quest'' abridged audiobook]] ==Sources== *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Dean Jinzler |pl=Dean Jinzler |fi=Dean Jinzler |deFA=1 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Jinzler, Dean}} [[Category:Car'das members]] [[Category:Coruscanti]] [[Category:Empire of the Hand citizens]] [[Category:Galactic Republic individuals]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Technicians]]
